planets.name: Aethon Pyralis
planets.desc: Binary tidal-locked to Aethon with asymmetric magma hemispheres. The dayside is a blinding ocean of molten ferrosilicate, while the nightside endures silicate rainstorms that freeze into obsidian spires. Subsurface magma tides create rhythmic seismic pulses that resonate across the entire mantle.

planets.name: Aethon Chlora
planets.desc: A chlorophyll-analog biosphere blankets this world in bioluminescent forests spanning entire continents. Alien flora photosynthesize using a dual-layered atmosphere that filters Aethon's radiation into two spectral bands. At night, the forests glow emerald and teal, visible from orbit as pulsing continental outlines.

planets.name: Aethon Zephyros
planets.desc: A violet gas giant with counter-rotating atmospheric bands of ammonia-phosphine clouds. Three nested storm vortices orbit each other in a gravitational waltz. Purple haze from tholin particles gives the upper atmosphere an ethereal glow, and violet-hued rings of captured cometary debris encircle the equator.

planets.name: Aethon Kryos
planets.desc: Cryovolcanic geysers of liquid nitrogen erupt through a fractured ice shell kilometres thick. A subsurface ammonia ocean harbors chemosynthetic ecosystems near hydrothermal seeps. The extreme axial tilt creates seasons where entire hemispheres thaw into slush plains, and frost fractals grow to kilometre scales in the long polar winters.

planets.name: Aethon Boreas
planets.desc: A sideways-spinning ice giant where diamond hail precipitates from methane-rich cloud decks into a mantle of metallic hydrogen. Supersonic methane jet streams carve dark bands across the blue atmosphere. The magnetic pole wanders chaotically, generating auroral storms that engulf entire hemispheres. Deep-blue rings of nitrogen ice orbit at a steep inclination to the ecliptic.

planets.name: Aethon Eremos
planets.desc: Ancient petrified dune seas stretch across this parched world, their silicate crests frozen in time. Subsurface aquifer networks detected by seismic echoes hint at a drowned past. Dust devils reaching the mesosphere carve ephemeral channels visible from orbit, and electrostatic discharges in dust clouds produce an eerie violet glow at twilight.

planets.name: Aethon Pelagos
planets.desc: A 200-kilometre-deep ocean with no detectable floor. Acoustic anomalies suggest macro-organisms of extraordinary scale inhabiting the abyssal depths. Tidal bore waves 3 km tall sweep the equatorial band, driven by resonant gravitational coupling with its moon. Bioluminescent blooms illuminate entire ocean basins in pulsing cerulean waves.

planets.name: Aethon Iovis
planets.desc: Near-retrograde rotation makes this toxic hellscape unique in the Aethon system. Phosphoric acid rain etches alien patterns into basalt plateaus while volcanic lightning superbolts — visible from orbit — arc between towering sulfur plumes. The dense yellow-green atmosphere super-rotates, circling the planet in just three days despite its months-long rotation.

planets.name: Aethon Anthrax
planets.desc: Graphene plains and fullerene mountains define this dark, exotic world. A diamond core produces a seismic hum audible through surface-coupled resonance. Tar lakes pool beneath a hydrocarbon fog that refracts starlight into prismatic halos. The surface glitters with sharp faceted reflections — an alien landscape of pure elemental carbon.

planets.name: Aethon Sideros
planets.desc: Tidally stressed nickel-iron crust fractures and reforms in rhythmic tectonic cycles. Magnetic flux tubes channel plasma from Aethon directly to the poles, creating permanent auroral columns. A metallic hydrogen interior generates the strongest magnetic field in the system, and polished metal plains reflect starlight like vast mirrors across the surface.

planets.name: Aethon Krystallos
planets.desc: Piezoelectric storms erupt from tectonic crystal stress as continent-sized quartz formations grind against each other. Prismatic light pillars rise from fracture zones, refracting Aethon's light into spectral columns visible across the horizon. Subsurface resonance chambers amplify seismic waves into harmonic frequencies that ring through the crystalline mantle.

planets.name: Aethon Thyella
planets.desc: A roiling storm colossus with perpetual hypercane vortices wider than terrestrial planets. Counter-rotating wind bands of dark indigo and pale violet churn at 900 km/h. Electrical discharges flash through ammonia ice crystals in continent-sized thunderheads, and deep gravity waves propagate through the atmosphere as visible ripple patterns spanning thousands of kilometres.

planets.name: Aethon Kryovulos
planets.desc: Geyser eruptions of volatile compounds blast through a cracked and fissured ice shell. Glowing cyan fissures trace a network of tidal heating across the pale terrain. Plumes of nitrogen and methane ice crystals arc gracefully in the low gravity, forming transient ring arcs that dissipate within hours. Subsurface convection cells reshape the surface on decadal timescales.

planets.name: Aethon Theron
planets.desc: No solid surface exists on this sphere of magnetically confined plasma. Electromagnetic filaments spanning thousands of kilometres writhe and reconnect in violent flares. Radio pulses detectable system-wide emanate from plasma oscillation modes. The extreme axial tilt causes its magnetic topology to precess chaotically, driving perpetual coronal mass ejections into the surrounding space.

planets.name: Aethon Mykes
planets.desc: A planet-spanning fungal neural network exhibits collective intelligence signals detected as coherent electromagnetic pulses. Bioluminescent fruiting bodies visible from orbit pulse in synchronized waves across entire hemispheres. Spore clouds periodically form temporary rings that orbit for weeks before dissipating. The mycelial network reaches kilometres deep, and seismic data suggests the entire crust is more organism than geology.
